title: 3D CG in Rebuild split
wikitext:
[]: 3D CG in Rebuild [split]             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Mon Apr 06, 2009 10:58 pm [Split from hereabouts .] Seems like that battle is going to look beautiful in 2.0..So much CG but that is alright if they do it right.

[]:             Quote Post by Joppu » Tue Apr 07, 2009 12:02 pm The CGI looks way different from the first movie. Now it look clean and crisp unlike the first movie's film grain stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Apr 07, 2009 12:12 pm I thought 1.0 and 1.01 did an amazing job on the CG.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 07, 2009 12:14 pm What CGI are you folks referring to?

[]:             Quote Post by Joppu » Tue Apr 07, 2009 12:47 pm Reichu wrote: What CGI are you folks referring to?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 07, 2009 1:11 pm Keep in mind that you're comparing a low-res, heavily compressed trailer (2.0) with, I'm assuming, a DVD release (1.1). When we were analyzing the 1.0 trailers way back, a lot of stuff looked way more "CG" than it ended up being.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Tue Apr 07, 2009 1:16 pm Joppu wrote: Reichu wrote: What CGI are you folks referring to?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Apr 07, 2009 1:28 pm The footage in the trailers are most likely two passes away from the completed image (for 2.0, 2.01 and beyond is another story). It's a general problem with CG in trailers. CG is a highly detailed and long winded process of revision revision revision. Very rarely are CG shots fully realized when they're shown in a trailer. When they are it's because the shots are the publics first images of popular characters and so forth. Using live action movies as a template, more often than not if you compare the CG available in the first trailer against those same shots in the final released film you'll see a difference in quality. I have no doubt all CG in 2.0 will look vastly more impressive in the (first) final product.

[]:             Quote Post by Joppu » Tue Apr 07, 2009 1:55 pm But yeah, the 1.0's CGI looks highly detailed and hand drawn while 2.0's trailer's CGI looks almost like cel-shading type animation

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Apr 07, 2009 2:08 pm I'd wait till you have equal quality images of both films to compare. You're comparing a DVD quality image with a low quality image. The 2.0 image lacks details because of the file size. Just wait.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Tue Apr 07, 2009 2:59 pm Joppu wrote: But yeah, the 1.0's CGI looks highly detailed and hand drawn while 2.0's trailer's CGI looks almost like cel-shading type animation

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Tue Apr 07, 2009 3:12 pm still plenty of cg scenes that made me want to puke. when eva 1 is being transported to the launch pad for the first time, i have to hold back tears of agony. And in the 2.0 trailer we see cg pedestrians during one scene which really disappointed me. I'll make some screen shots of what i'm talking about in a bit

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 07, 2009 3:34 pm Axell wrote: still plenty of cg scenes that made me want to puke. when eva 1 is being transported to the launch pad for the first time, i have to hold back tears of agony.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Apr 07, 2009 6:52 pm Axell wrote: And in the 2.0 trailer we see cg pedestrians during one scene which really disappointed me.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Tue Apr 07, 2009 7:14 pm I couldnt tell personally, i guess it was decently done or i was just dozing off during the anno street and telephone post shots that went right into them as to the 2 scene i was referring to tho... http://i153.photobucket.com/albums/s202/Axellmds/vlcsnap-2705817.png eva1 looks like a toy here imo http://i153.photobucket.com/albums/s202/Axellmds/00001034.jpg this reminded me of the horrible cg armies in Utawarerumono

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Apr 07, 2009 7:17 pm Reichu wrote: That's just a glaring example of 3D CG fail in the movie. But I think 3D Shamshel is kind of an eyesore in general.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 07, 2009 7:40 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: I kinda liked 3D Shamshel. Then again that's just me. *expecting facepalm*

[]:             Quote Post by Lagann » Tue Apr 07, 2009 8:08 pm Reichu wrote: I just feel that Shammy isn't as visually well-integrated as she should be -- i.e., the CG sticks out, in that "LOOK AT ME, I'M 3D CG!!!!" kind of way

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Apr 07, 2009 8:22 pm Axell wrote: eva1 looks like a toy here imo

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Apr 07, 2009 10:50 pm Whow, I thought I had started a new topic by accident. Some CG can get a little funky at times. I hope they don't "over do" it in 2.0 and so on.
